results.title: Bligh of the Bounty: world navigator
results.creator_credit: Dennis Murphy, Bruce Harris and Rolf Harris Video Productions
results.brief_description: A four-part television documentary presented by Rolf Harris, with accompanying book, about the life and journeys of Captain William Bligh. Captain of the ill-fated Bounty, Bligh was also a navigator, surveyor and explorer. The series covers the story of Captain Bligh from his birth to his death, and follows his voyages from England through the Canary Islands, the Caribbean and Canada to the South Pacific. The 112 page book ‘Mutiny on the Bounty’ presents an in-depth historical survey of the Mutiny, its causes and its consequences through essays, articles and colour illustrations.

results.title: Blaze away
results.creator_credit: Hal Roach and Pathe Melody Comedies
results.brief_description: A silent slapstick comedy set in the wild West. An easterner goes west and gets the job as the local sheriff. He manages to capture the villain and then lock himself up! Starring Paul Parrott and Jobyna Ralston.

results.title: Black sun
results.creator_credit: Amanda Jane, Hugh Burton and Victorian College of the Arts
results.brief_description: The story begins and ends with a funeral. A group of female friends unite for a night of drugs, music and memories under the full moon. One of the girls has been away to the city, and discovers that things don’t stay the same, that friendships change, and can be challenged and proven very quickly. Cast includes Raelee Hill and Brigid Kelly.

results.title: Black Island
results.creator_credit: Ben Bolt, Carole K. Smith and Children's Film Foundation
results.brief_description: A children’s adventure film about two young boys, Michael and Joe, who find themselves stranded on a remote island with a couple of escaped convicts. Cast includes Martin Murphy, Mikael Salomon, Michael Elphick, Frank Leigh, Allan Surtees, Norman Tipton.

results.title: Black Fire (Blackfire)
results.brief_description: Blackfire is one of the first film ever directed by an Aboriginal person. It is a collection of connected ideas and a take on both the state of Anthropology and Aboriginal Affairs in 1972.

constellations_other.name: Jedda: Uneven ground, the road to on-screen diversity
constellations_other.description: Jedda was the first fully funded Australian feature to be shot on colour film. It was also the first Australian film with Aboriginal actors in lead roles, however, it reflects a deeply problematic time in Australian history.

constellations_other.name: Black Power through film and TV
constellations_other.description: The Black Power movement of the late 1960s and early 70s was “the most exciting and dynamic era in modern Indigenous history” according to Aboriginal activist Professor Gary Foley, who gained widespread attention for protests during the time and later leveraged film and TV to fight for Indigenous rights.

results.description:
Blackfire is one of the first film ever directed by an Aboriginal person. It is a collection of connected ideas and a take on both the state of Anthropology and Aboriginal Affairs in 1972.

Source: Black Fire (Blackfire) (Film) - Creative Spirits, retrieved from https://www.creativespirits.info/resources/movies/black-fire-blackfire

results.title: Black dogs
results.creator_credit: Andrew Sully, Luigi Acquisto and Victorian College of the Arts, School of Film and Television
results.brief_description: A short Melbourne comedy which explores the complications that arise when a gothic punk woman and her racist skinhead boyfriend move next door to a Vietnamese-Australian couple in Melbourne’s western suburbs. Funny and astute, “Black Dogs” pokes gentle fun at some of the eccentricities of youth subcultures without being patronising or offensive. Sharply written and directed, this gem of a movie manages to entertain as it examines some of the tensions of modern “multicultural” Australia. The acting by the young cast is faultless.

results.title: Bittersweet
results.brief_description: This charming short, based on an Aboriginal healing ritual, is about a young school girl, Ada, who attempts to hide her school report card from her Gran. When her Gran finds out, she takes Ada out to a nearby tree and introduces her not only to some magic but also to a moral lesson about attitude and outlook on life. Suitable for primary school children.
